title: Tarenna ebracteata
intro: Tarenna ebracteata is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Tarenna ebracteata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Tarenna ebracteata include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including bronchitis, sore throats, and digestive problems.
taxonomy: Tarenna ebracteata is a species of plant within the family Rubiaceae. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. Members of the Rubiaceae family are found in tropical and subtropical regions around the world. This family includes over 600 genera and approximately 13,000 species. Tarenna ebracteata is likely to be a part of the genus Tarenna, which is a group of flowering plants native to Asia.
